assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Each criterion has a dedicated output file (criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md) with 'Verdict: PASS' for: single license filter (criterion-1), comma-separated filter (criterion-2), 400 validation (criterion-3), pagination integration (criterion-4), unchanged response shape (criterion-5). The report's Acceptance Criteria section (lines 86-113) also details all 5 criteria as PASS with code-level evidence.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 files in the diff match the task specification exactly (2 modified, 1 created)'. The detailed Scope Containment findings (lines 26-34) list: Files to Modify: 'modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs' -- both present as modified; Files to Create: 'tests/api/package.rs' -- present as new; Out-of-scope files: none; Unimplemented files: none.

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments or review body items on this PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. No sub-task creation is mentioned anywhere in any output file. The outputs contain no evidence of sub-task creation.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The output files are report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md — all are verification reports/analysis documents. None of the output files contain code modifications or patches. The report describes reading and analyzing the diff but never mentions editing, writing, or modifying any source files. All findings are read-only observations of the diff content.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report concludes (line 17) with 'Overall: PASS' and a summary of findings. There is no mention of merging, approving, or any automated merge action anywhere in any output file. The report presents detailed findings with evidence for human review.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, API keys, passwords, private keys, or credentials detected in added lines'. The detailed Security Findings section (lines 60-69) provides file-by-file evidence: list.rs contains Rust imports and validation logic, service/mod.rs contains filter/join logic, tests/api/package.rs contains test setup with SPDX identifiers. Explicitly states: 'No .env files, no connection strings with embedded passwords, no API key patterns (AKIA, sk-, ghp_, etc.), no PEM key blocks.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test file added (tests/api/package.rs); no modified or deleted test files'. The detailed Test Change Classification section (lines 165-172) confirms: 'tests/api/package.rs: new file adding 4 test functions and ~80 lines of test code', 'No other test files appear in the diff', 'No test functions removed, no assertions relaxed, no skip annotations added'.

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: The report summary table (lines 5-15) contains all expected verdicts: Review Feedback (N/A), Root-Cause Investigation (N/A), Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (N/A), Sensitive Patterns (PASS), CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Test Quality (PASS), Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE), Verification Commands (N/A). The detailed findings are organized by domain: 'Intent Alignment Findings' (lines 23-54) covering Scope Containment, Diff Size, Commit Traceability; 'Security Findings' (lines 58-70) covering Sensitive Pattern Scan; 'Correctness Findings' (lines 74-118) covering CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, Verification Commands; 'Style/Conventions Findings' (lines 122-172) covering Convention Upgrade, Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, Eval Quality, Test Change Classification. Test Quality in the summary (line 13) combines Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality (N/A).

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 26-34): file-by-file scope comparison listing each file from task spec and confirming presence in diff ('modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs' as modified, 'tests/api/package.rs' as new). Security (lines 64-68): file-by-file pattern scanning with specific patterns checked ('list.rs: Added lines contain Rust imports...', 'service/mod.rs: Added lines contain...', 'No .env files, no connection strings... no API key patterns (AKIA, sk-, ghp_...)'. Correctness (lines 86-113): per-criterion verification with code-level evidence for all 5 acceptance criteria, citing specific functions, code snippets, and test names. Style/Conventions (lines 134-153): test quality assessment examining each of 4 test functions individually with specific descriptions of their setup, queries, and assertions.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | All 4 test functions have doc comments; no repetitive test patterns detected...; Eval Quality: N/A'. The detailed Eval Quality section (lines 158-162): 'Eval Quality -- N/A', 'Details: No eval result reviews found on this PR. No github-actions[bot] reviews with ## Eval Results marker detected.' The Test Quality verdict is PASS based on Repetitive Test Detection (PASS) and Test Documentation (PASS), with Eval Quality not affecting the combination.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 158-162: 'No eval result reviews found on this PR'). No sub-tasks of any kind are created — no mention of sub-task creation appears in any output file. The Root-Cause Investigation row (line 6) states 'N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate', confirming no sub-tasks were created for any reason including eval failures.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments or review body items on this PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. These two verdicts appear in the summary table (lines 5-6) before the domain findings sections (which start at line 23 with 'Intent Alignment Findings'). They are not nested under any domain heading, indicating they are determined at the orchestrator level independently from the four domain sub-agents.
